sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Julia Jones, a 25-year-old female, presents for evaluation of breast reduction due to symptomatic macromastia.
She has been experiencing back pain, neck pain, and discomfort due to her large breasts, which she attributes to her breast size, especially when working at her desk.
She has been considering breast reduction for approximately 6-7 years and has seen a chiropractor for back pain.
She has a history of bra strap indentations and occasionally experiences numbness or tingling in her hands.
She has not experienced rashes underneath her breasts.
She takes a migraine medication, propranolol, and has a history of a family member with breast cancer, with her mother being diagnosed at age 40.
She is currently a student, studying psychology, and drinks wine occasionally.
She does not use tobacco or drugs.
Past medical history: History of family member with breast cancer, mother diagnosed at age 40.
Review of systems: The patient reports back pain, neck pain, numbness or tingling in her hands, and occasional nipple pain.
Current medications: Propranolol 1 tablet [frequency not specified] for migraine.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.

Physical exam: The patient's physical exam findings reveal asymmetrical breasts with severe ptosis bilaterally, no nipple retraction or discharge bilaterally, no breast tenderness, masses, or axillary lymphadenopathy is palpable.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is an ideal candidate for bilateral reduction mammoplasty due to symptomatic macromastia.
Problem list: Macromastia; active; confirmed; Symptomatic back pain; active; confirmed; Numbness or tingling in hands; active; confirmed; Family history of breast cancer; past; confirmed.
Plan:
I will document the patient's symptoms and measurements, and send them to her insurance company for approval.
I will take photos of the patient's breasts and provide her with information about the surgery, including the incision site, drainage, and post-operative care.
I will schedule a follow-up appointment with the patient to discuss any last-minute questions and go over instructions in more detail.
I will also inform the patient that she will need to have a clear plastic tape over her incision and that the drains will be removed in the clinic after 1 week.
I will advise her to avoid heavy lifting, driving, and strenuous activities for 6 weeks post-operatively.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss any last-minute questions and go over instructions in more detail.
